description: Compound peer-key (maturity_stage, sector_group [, tech_sub_bucket]). Used for cohort-relative percentile computation on Adoption + Financial pillars. Curated to avoid bimodal cohorts where naive (sector, maturity) splits would produce small/noisy peer groups. v1.1.0 splits the original tech_hardware (semis + AAPL/CSCO/SMCI) into curated Hardware / Semiconductors / Software / IT Services sub-buckets to close the AAPL bimodality (docs/lthcs-tech-hardware-software-split.md). See docs/peer-group-audit.md §3.4 and docs/lthcs-open-items-audit.md Tier 2 row 7 for the rationale.

tech_hardware.description: Hardware platforms: consumer devices (AAPL), networking gear (CSCO), AI server OEM (SMCI). Curated split out of the original semi+hardware cohort.
tech_hardware.rationale: Revenue from hardware product cycles (iPhone, switching, AI servers). Distinct from semi silicon cycle. Cohort n=3 intentionally falls below min_cohort_size floor and cascades to maturity_only via the resolver — that is the audit-prescribed behaviour to avoid the AAPL-vs-NVDA bimodality.

tech_semiconductors.description: Silicon manufacturers + design + foundry + wafer-fab equipment
tech_semiconductors.rationale: Revenue tied to silicon cycle: AI / datacenter / mobile / analog / memory. Margin profiles dominated by foundry gross margin + share gains. Cycle-bimodal across maturity stages (NVDA/MU at peak vs INTC recovery); the maturity_stage axis handles this via the resolver's cascade.

tech_software.rationale: Revenue growth distribution centered ~10-25%. Subscription / SaaS revenue models. Cybersecurity (CRWD/FTNT/PANW/ZS) and EDA (CDNS/SNPS) roll in — subscription economics and growth distribution match Software.

tech_it_services.description: IT consulting, integration, outsourcing, distribution / managed services
tech_it_services.rationale: Revenue from services / consulting / reseller economics — gross margins and growth profile distinct from product Software. Cohort n=4 intentionally falls below min_cohort_size floor and cascades to maturity_only.

tech_internet.description: Consumer internet, ad-supported platforms, e-commerce, online travel/delivery, streaming, gaming
tech_internet.rationale: Revenue tied to ad cycle, take-rate dynamics, consumer engagement. Distinct from B2B software.

communication_telecom.rationale: Sub-driven revenue + ad-cycle dependence; mature, low-growth.

consumer_staples.rationale: Revenue growth 2-5%; volume-driven; pricing power varies.

consumer_cyclical.description: Discretionary spending exposure: retail, restaurants, autos, hotels, apparel
consumer_cyclical.rationale: Revenue growth tracks employment + housing + consumer discretionary cycle. Internet-native discretionary (AMZN, BKNG, MELI, etc.) lives in tech_internet.

healthcare_pharma.rationale: Revenue tied to drug-cycle (Humira biosimilar, GLP-1, oncology pipelines).

healthcare_devices.rationale: Revenue + margins dominated by procedure volume + reimbursement. Managed care (UNH) included — revenue dynamics tied to healthcare utilisation rather than drug pipeline.

industrials_aero.description: Aerospace, defense, machinery, transports, diversified industrials, business services
industrials_aero.rationale: Multi-year capex cycle; backlog visibility; freight + capex demand. HR/payroll services (ADP, PAYX) + data analytics (CSGP, VRSK) classified as Industrials in the universe and fit reasonably here.

energy.rationale: Revenue dominated by commodity prices; sensitive to oil + gas regime. Small cohort — expect fallback to maturity-only when stage splits this further.

utilities_reits.rationale: Slow growth; dividend stability is the signal. Grouped together because both are interest-rate-sensitive yield names.

banks.rationale: Already a special-case cohort in financial.py for NII / PCL / noninterest income. Surfaced here so callers that DON'T route through the bank path still see the right peer cohort.

financials_non_bank.description: Payments, asset managers, insurance, brokers, diversified financials
financials_non_bank.rationale: Revenue + economics very different from banks. Payments (V, MA, AXP, PYPL) and asset managers (BLK, SCHW) sit closer to software-like scale economics than bank NII dynamics.

materials.rationale: Small cohort (2 tickers). Will fall back to maturity-only via safety valve.

other.description: Fallback for tickers not curated into a sector_group. Currently empty — every universe ticker is curated.
other.rationale: Defined for completeness so new universe additions land somewhere; safety valve fallback chain still applies.

adoption_momentum.comment: Revenue growth varies wildly across sectors; cohort-relative is essential.

institutional_confidence.comment: Audit (peer-group-audit §3.4) said KEEP universe-relative for momentum. Sector-relative would over-correct for sector-rotation cycles.

financial_evolution.comment: Banks have their own cohort (commit e456582). Non-banks use compound key.

thesis_integrity.comment: Sentiment + analyst consensus + 8-K events are absolute signals, not relative.

des.comment: DES already maps sector -> DES weight; the sector_group provides the right cohort lens.

active_overrides.comment: When true, lthcs_daily.py routes peer-cohort lookups through the declarative config. When false, current hard-coded logic in pillars runs unchanged. Default OFF so V1 production behavior is preserved.
